title: potentilla hispanica subsp. oreodoxa
intro: Potentilla hispanica subsp. oreodoxa is a subspecies of the Potentilla hispanica plant, a type of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Potentilla hispanica subsp. oreodoxa include the potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties, making it useful in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, and it can also be dried and used in teas or capsules.
